subject: Computer Networking
title: MIME and Attachments
description: A quick, practical guide to MIME and Attachments.

hook.text: Email was designed for plain text. MIME is the hack that lets you send images, PDFs, and HTML.

buildup.text: MIME — Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions — wraps non-text content with type labels and encoding.

discovery.text: Base64 encoding converts binary files to ASCII text. The receiver decodes it back to the original file.

twist.text: A single email can have multiple MIME parts: plain text, HTML version, and attachments side by side.

climax.text: Content-Type headers tell the client what each part is: text/plain, image/png, application/pdf.

punchline.text: MIME makes email multimedia. Text protocol, rich content.

quiz.question: What encoding does MIME use to send binary files as text?
